questionText: Particle A has position $x = 2t$, $y = t^2$ and particle B has position $x = 12 - t$, $y = 9$ (metres, $t$ in seconds). Determine whether the particles collide, and if so, when and where.

steps.workingLatex: x_A = x_B \text{ and } y_A = y_B \text{ at the same } t
steps.explanation: A collision requires both coordinates to match at the same instant, not just the paths to cross.
Setting $y_A = y_B$ gives $t = 3$ (taking the physically sensible positive time).

steps.description: Check the x-coordinates at that time
steps.explanation: At $t = 3$, particle A is at $x = 6$ and particle B is at $x = 9$.
The $x$-coordinates differ at $t = 3$, so the particles are not at the same point.
Since there is no time when both coordinates agree, the particles do not collide.

workedSolution.finalAnswer: The particles do not collide (at $t = 3$ they share $y$ but not $x$).
